data.title: U.S. Mortgage Rates Remain Below 6.5% as Oil Decline Eases Inflation Concerns
data.description:
The average U.S. 30-year fixed-rate mortgage (FRM) sits at 6.43%, down from 6.49% a week ago, said Freddie Mac (FMCC) on Thursday.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Oil and gas prices have tumbled lower since the U.S. and Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. That's taken pressure off inflation, which in turn can bring down Treasury yields. However, the 10-year Treasury yield, which the 30-year FRM tracks, has actually surged 10.9 basis points over the past week. The yield is currently at 4.485%.

Mortgage Rates Slip Despite Higher Yields

This reflects a disconnect between mortgage rates and the 10-year yield, as the rate fell despite higher yields. The divergence suggests mortgage rates may not fall much further in the near term if the relationship between the two rates holds.

One potential explanation backing the divergence is that inflation expectations remain elevated even as energy prices decline. This could keep longer-term yields high and the Fed cautious, limiting the downside of mortgage rates.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: "30-Plus Years of NASA Research…," Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Gains as JetZero Advances
data.description:
Aerospace stock Boeing (BA) has enjoyed its position as half of a duopoly for several years now. But reports suggest that position may be threatened as JetZero is starting to advance in the field. Even with this potential new competitive threat, Boeing is still holding shareholder interest. In fact, shareholders gave Boeing shares a nearly 3% boost in Thursday afternoon's trading.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Reports note that JetZero is currently working out in the Mojave Desert on a "full-size demonstrator" model of a new blended-wing commercial jet that could represent a fundamental shift in the way airlines do business. In fact, reports note, the JetZero plane is set to be a 200-plus-seat jet, which is a major market segment that is a big part of not only Boeing's future strategies, but also those of Airbus (EADSY).

Tom O'Leary, JetZero's CEO, noted, "Nobody's ever done this before. We're taking existing technology, 30-plus years of NASA research." The original plans were greeted with no small skepticism, but the farther along JetZero got, the more people started paying attention. There are still doubts, of course. Some think the blended-wing design is better suited to military aircraft than passenger aircraft. And others noted that the projected fuel savings—potentially 50% against current tube-fuselage aircraft—had yet to be proven. Still, this is a huge potential competitor, if all goes as planned.

IT Outage

IT outages are nothing new for most businesses. And Boeing proved to be no exception here, suffering an outage itself at one of the worst possible times: the last day of the quarter. Boeing still managed to complete some deliveries, but the IT outages brought some paperwork and final inspections to an unfortunate halt.

Thankfully, the outage was not a cyberattack, Boeing reps noted, and it was in the process of coming back online even as Boeing released statements. It was to be a mild hiccup, though it came at a terrible time and will likely put a small crimp in the final numbers for the quarter.

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 15 Buys and one Hold assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 1.23% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $274.46 per share implies 21.89% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: VOO ETF Surges on Weaker Labor Market Data Amid Tech Stock Drag
data.description:
The Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO) climbed on Thursday as investors reacted positively to the latest U.S. labor market report. The fund had fallen by over 0.12% in premarket trading before rebounding after the market opened as tech stocks, particularly chipmakers, stabilized. Meanwhile, investors are still cautious as uncertainty remains over the Federal Reserve's next interest rate decision.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

NVDS: built for a short position on NVDA

U.S. Labor Report, Lower Oil Prices Lift VOO ETF

The Vanguard S&P 500 ETF rose about 0.8% immediately after the market opened on Thursday, trading near $687 before reversing those gains during later trading sessions. The fund is also up 9.97% year to date, outperforming its sector category's 5.14%.

The rebound came after a weaker-than-expected June labor market report lifted investor sentiment. The U.S. economy added 57,000 jobs during the month, well below economists' forecast of 115,000. The softer data eased expectations of a July Fed interest rate hike, boosting the broader market.

Meanwhile, falling oil prices supported the rally. Brent crude slipped toward $70 a barrel after progress in indirect talks between the U.S. and Iran eased worries over potential supply disruptions. Lower energy prices also reduced inflation worries, improving the outlook for companies in the S&P 500.

Tech stocks also recovered after Wednesday's sell-off in the semiconductor and AI sector. The rebound in major holdings, including Micron Technology (MU) and Apple (AAPL), lifted the market-cap-weighted S&P 500 and drove further gains in VOO.

VOO ETF Remains Under Pressure Despite Rebound

Despite VOO's uptick, investors still remain cautious after Fed officials reaffirmed their commitment to bringing inflation back to the 2% target. That has reinforced expectations that borrowing costs could stay higher for longer, keeping pressure on VOO and the broader U.S. stock market.

Moreover, previous profit-taking in tech stocks added to VOO's earlier weakness. After strong gains in tech, AI, and semiconductor stocks during the first half of the year, many investors locked in profits, triggering a broad sell-off across the chip sector.

The impact on VOO was significant because it tracks the S&P 500 using a market-cap-weighted approach, giving its largest holdings the greatest influence on performance. Tech firms account for most of the fund's top holdings, so recent losses in major names such as Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) continue to weigh on the ETF.

Is VOO a Good ETF Investment?

Based on Wall Street analysts' consensus on TipRanks, VOO ETF currently carries a Strong Buy rating. The fund also has an average price target of $835, implying about 22.43% upside. (See VOO ETF's Forecast)
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Anthropic Relaunches Nerfed Fable 5 and Secretly Flags Developers as "Too Dumb"
data.description:
Claude chatbot creator Anthropic has officially reactivated its Fable 5 AI model after the U.S. Commerce Department lifted its export ban on the tool. The relaunch has been met with mixed reactions from developers on social media platforms like X. While some praised its capabilities, others were furious after leaked system logs revealed that Anthropic was secretly downgrading their requests without telling them

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Critics Say Anthropic's Reactivated Fable 5 Was Nerfed

Anthropic added a new safety rail to its model that now blocks a specific jailbreak technique in more than 99% of cases. However, the stricter filter comes at a cost. Some common coding and debugging requests are now being flagged, with many users expressing concerns.

While some users on X welcome the extra safeguards and see them as a fair price for getting the model back after its export ban. Others call the new guardrails overly cautious, saying Anthropic swapped one problem for another by making the model too quick to refuse harmless prompts.

BridgeMind, an agentic coding platform, has criticized Anthropic for its new guardrails. The firm said the AI company released a "nerfed" version of Fable 5. BridgeMind also noted that Fable 5's debugging success score went from 86.2% before the ban to 25.9% after it went live again. The model's refactoring and hallucination score also became much worse, falling 73.6% to 38.4% and 75.9% to 61.7%, respectively. 

Developers Slam Anthropic After Downgrading Models

Developers on X also said that Fable 5's guardrails are being too sensitive. According to them, normal coding work is being flagged as potentially harmful, with the model's safety filter panicking and assuming they are trying to build cyberweapons. As a result, these tasks are being dumped back to the older Opus 4.8 model.

One such case came from Dax, a developer working on the open-source coding tool OpenCode. He shared system logs showing that his normal requests on Fable 5 were being quietly downgraded to Opus 4.8. 

The log flag read: TOO_DUMB_TO_NEED_FABLE, meaning Anthropic's system had decided his tasks were not complex enough to require the premium model.

After Dax posted the logs, Claude Code engineer Thariq Shihipar responded: "Honestly, I didn't expect you to look at the logs." The comment sparked backlash on social media, with developers taking it as proof that Anthropic did not expect paying users to notice that it had switched Fable 5 to a downgraded, cheaper model.

Thariq later clarified that the flag was part of an anti-distillation experiment from March and said it would be rolled back. However, by that point, developers' trust had already been damaged.

Is It a Good Time to Invest in Anthropic?

Anthropic is still a private company with no publicly traded shares yet. However, investors seeking exposure to AI stocks can consider the Best Artificial Intelligence Stocks tracked by TipRanks.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Martin Marietta Materials Stock (MLM) Motors on New M&A and Revenue Growth Hopes
data.description:
Shares in materials business Martin Marietta Materials (MLM) were higher today after a leading analyst said its proposed acquisition of Lhoist North American Lime operations could be the spark for further M&A.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Deal Makes Strategic Sense

Four-Star TipRanks-rated analyst Anthony Codling of RBC Capital Markets said that although the proposed $13.5 billion deal was slightly expensive, he believes it makes strategic sense.

The brokerage said it estimates the deal would increase its 2027 revenue and EPS forecasts by 24% and 4%, respectively, assuming the transaction closes by the end of 2026 and half of the expected synergies are realized.

Martin Marietta is likely to extract far more value from the more than 2 billion tonnes of limestone reserves acquired through the deal than Lhoist currently does, according to Codling's analysis.

He added that a medium-term bull case for the acquisition can be justified given a more diversified revenue profile, potential upside to cost and revenue synergies and greater M&A opportunities going forward.

Martin Marietta, which mines and supplies crushed stone, sand and gravel, and produces downstream products like ready-mixed concrete, asphalt and cement, plans to fund the transaction with $7 billion in cash along with shares of its stock valued at $6.5 billion.

Manufacturing and Energy Demand

Lhoist North America, based in Fort Worth, Texas, is part of privately held Lhoist Group of Belgium. The limestone supplier operates 20 quarries and production facilities in North America. It generated $1.8 billion in gross sales and $786 million of Adjusted EBITDA for the twelve months ended December 31, 2025. The transaction is expected to be completed in the second half of this year.

The deal will position Martin Marietta to capture growing demand from advanced manufacturing, infrastructure and energy construction work, said Martin Marietta Chief Executive Officer Ward Nye.

Martin Marietta expects to realize approximately $85 million in annual run-rate cost synergies, with additional potential upside from commercial and operational opportunities. The transaction is expected to be accretive3 to earnings and margins in the first full year following close.

Is MLM a Good Stock to Buy Now?

On TipRanks, MLM has a Moderate Buy consensus based on 8 Buy and 6 Hold ratings. Its highest price target is $745. MLM stock's consensus price target is $672.17, implying a 13.48% upside. (See MLM's Stock Forecast).
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Down Today, 7/2/26?
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) opened higher on Thursday but have since reversed course, falling into negative territory.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The culprit behind the reversal is June's jobs report. During the month, the U.S. economy added 57,000 jobs, well below the consensus estimate of 110,000 additions. Making matters worse, April's and May's payrolls were revised lower by a combined 74,000 jobs.

Furthermore, the unemployment rate fell to a one-year low of 4.2% during the month. That appears to be positive on the surface. However, the fall was attributed to the labor force participation rate dropping to 61.5%. This indicates a smaller share of people were working or looking for work.

AI Sector Weakens as Investors Shift Into Software and Financials

Meanwhile, memory and semiconductor stocks continue to face pressure. Micron (MU), AMD (AMD), Intel (INTC), and Lam Research (LRCX) are all down by over 5% at the time of writing. In recent days, investors have aggressively realized profits from AI-beneficiaries following significant year-to-date gains.

Some of that capital has rotated into beaten-down software and financial stocks. These include Palantir (PLTR), Adobe (ADBE), Visa (V), and Mastercard (MA).
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: "81 Million Login Attempts": Microsoft Stock (NASDAQ:MSFT) Gains Following Huge Brute Force Attack
data.description:
Tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) is no stranger to cybersecurity issues. With so many platforms that people use every day, protecting those platforms from unwanted intrusion is basically second nature. And Microsoft managed to fend off one doozy of an attack recently, as a "password-spraying campaign" fired up only recently. The attack, and subsequent fact that little damage was done, was welcome news to shareholders, who gave Microsoft shares nearly a 2% boost in Thursday afternoon's trading.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

The attack targeted Microsoft 365, reports noted, and featured 81 million login attempts spread out over a period of two weeks. The person behind the threat tried using command-line interface (CLI) systems with still-valid username and password combinations that were found from past system breaches. By then, quite a few of those had likely been changed, but some were not, and that opened up an opportunity for the hacker to use the Resource Owner Password Credentials (ROPC) O Auth system.

That in turn allowed the hacker to bypass multi-factor authentication (MFA) in at least some cases. And with that came some key realizations about MFA in cybersecurity, and some of its key limitations. In some cases, reports noted, MFA had been applied, but only to certain applications as opposed to all cloud apps. MFA also may not have been enforced for some user groups, like regular end users, as opposed to administrators.

The Disc-to-Digital Conundrum

This generation—and to a limited extent the last generation—has given rise to a particular problem for gamers. When you buy a game on disc, why do you not have the ability to download the game from the Xbox store? This is particularly important if the disc should be unplayable due to scratches or the like. But Microsoft is currently testing a tool that may help on that front, albeit too late for a lot of gamers who already bought digital copies of games they already owned on disc.

The feature is currently known as "Disc2Digital," reports note, and that may well change by the time it goes live. Reports also note that it will work for Xbox One and Xbox Series X discs only, so those hoping to digitize their old Xbox and Xbox 360 titles will be, sadly, out of luck.

Is Microsoft a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on MSFT stock based on 35 Buys and one Hold assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 22.97% loss in its share price over the past year, the average MSFT price target of $562.10 per share implies 43.53% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Plunges as Semi Has its First Fatal Accident
data.description:
It was likely only a matter of time, but the Tesla Semi from electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) was involved in its first-ever fatal accident recently. The accident left two people dead and a legion of questions in its wake. It also left some very unhappy shareholders, who abandoned their positions and dealt Tesla more than an 8% loss in its share price in Thursday afternoon's trading.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

200% short exposure to TSLA with TSLQ

The accident happened on U.S. 50 in Nevada, reports noted, and the driver of the Tesla Semi apparently fell asleep before driving into two passenger vehicles that had stopped at a red light. Two of the occupants of the passenger vehicles were killed at the scene, and a third person had been flown to a local hospital with "life-threatening injuries."

Tesla Semi trucks do not come with Full Self-Driving (Supervised) capability, reports note. That capability is actually still in testing phases, and a Tesla Semi with FSD test hardware was recently spotted out in California. But there are safety systems that could have stepped in, like automatic emergency braking (AEB) that detect vehicles and other obstacles and brake automatically. Most modern semis have AEB installed, and some new laws are in the works that require them universally. But Tesla has never published safety specs about the Semi, reports note, so we do not know if AEB is in place therein.

Smile! You're on Cabin Camera!

Meanwhile, Tesla drivers may have a new hoop to jump through to use FSD: the cabin camera. The FSD system already uses the cabin camera to confirm that drivers are, indeed, supervising the system. But new reports suggest the cabin camera may go even farther, putting FSD behind a "biometric lock."

A recent deep dive into the Tesla mobile app version 4.58.5 suggests that Tesla wants to put the cabin camera to use in such fashion. There are specific entries in the code relating to "identity check failed" messages and dialog. This actually dovetails well with earlier software updates suggesting that the Tesla cabin camera might start checking driver age with facial analysis. It is not a far cry to go from "checking your age" to "checking to make sure you are this person."

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 11 Buys, 15 Holds, and three Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 34.87% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $404.86 per share implies 3.67% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: S&P 500 Faces Risk of a 'Three-Wave Correction,' Warns Bank of America
data.description:
Paul Ciana, Bank of America's Global Head of Technical Strategy, believes that the S&P 500 (SPX) could face a "three-wave correction" in the coming months. A three-wave correction is a decline that occurs in three stages before the market stabilizes. The correction could bring the index to as low as 6,850, implying downside of 8.5% from current levels.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

"Be cautious if a marginal new high toward ~7,741 occurs as it may be a 'bull trap' consistent with an expanding flat," wrote Ciana. A bull trap refers to a false breakout that reverses lower, while an expanding flat is a technical pattern where price swings get bigger over time instead of tightening.

Ciana Warns of Stretched Positioning as BofA Maintains Bearish Outlook

Ciana believes that price action appears "stretched," while optimism following the U.S.-Iran ceasefire extension is fading. In addition, Ciana notes that investors should take a defensive stance from July through September.

Earlier this week, Bank of America reiterated its 2026 S&P 500 price target of 7,100. The target implies downside of 5% and is the lowest forecast on Wall Street. The firm cited cooling liquidity drivers, including lower buybacks, dovish central banks, and falling institutional demand.
data.author: Eddie Pan
